title: austrostipa scabra subsp. falcata
intro: Austrostipa scabra subsp. falcata, also known as the rough blady grass, is a plant species native to Australia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Austrostipa scabra subsp. falcata is often made into teas, infusions, or poultices to be used topically or internally for its medicinal and health benefits.
